Then create a `<div id=\"terminal\"></div>` onto which xterm can attach itself. Finally instantiate the `Terminal` object and then call the `open` function with the DOM object of the `div`.\n\n```\n<!doctype html>\n  <html>\n    <head>\n      <link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"node_modules/@xterm/xterm/css/xterm.css\" />\n      <script src=\"node_modules/@xterm/xterm/lib/xterm.js\"></script>\n    </head>\n    <body>\n      <div id=\"terminal\"></div>\n      <script>\n        var term = new Terminal();\n        term.open(document.getElementById('terminal'));\n        term.write('Hello from \\x1B[1;3;31mxterm.js\\x1B[0m $ ')\n      </script>\n    </body>\n  </html>\n```\n\n## Real-world uses\n\nXterm.js is used in several world-class applications to provide great terminal experiences.\n\nReal-world uses\n\n-   [**SourceLair**](https://www.sourcelair.com/): In-browser IDE that provides its users with fully-featured Linux terminals based on xterm.js.\n-   [**Microsoft Visual Studio Code**](http://code.visualstudio.com/): Modern, versatile, and powerful open source code editor that provides an integrated terminal based on xterm.js.\n-   [**ttyd**](https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd): A command-line tool for sharing terminal over the web, with fully-featured terminal emulation based on xterm.js.\n-   [**Katacoda**](https://www.katacoda.com/): Katacoda is an Interactive Learning Platform for software developers, covering the latest Cloud Native technologies.\n-   [**Eclipse Che**](http://www.eclipse.org/che): Developer workspace server, cloud IDE, and Eclipse next-generation IDE.\n-   [**Codenvy**](http://www.codenvy.com): Cloud workspaces for development teams.\n-   [**CoderPad**](https://coderpad.io): Online interviewing platform for programmers. /Users/johnlindquist/.local/share/opencode/storage/part/msg_b4cbd9a37002BVroxJSgC73BDl/prt_b4cbd9f2a0010MJ8DnRrrrdkqt.json:12:      "content": "---\ntitle: \"Building AI Agents That Never Fail: DurableAgent, Recovery Patterns, and Resilience Architecture\"\ndate: 2025-12-23\nauthor: \"Claude Code\"\ntags: [\"agents\", \"durability\", \"resilience\", \"error-recovery\", \"production\"]\nexcerpt: \"Learn how to architect AI agents with bulletproof recovery patterns, automatic retries, and persistent state management. Discover the DurableAgent pattern that powers production systems.\"\n---\n\n# Building AI Agents That Never Fail: DurableAgent, Recovery Patterns, and Resilience Architecture\n\nBuilding AI agents is deceptively simple until you hit production. A prompt loop works great in notebooks. But in production, agents face network timeouts, LLM rate limits, hallucinations, infinite loops, and state corruption. The difference between a toy agent and a production agent is **durability**—the ability to survive failures, resume work, and maintain correctness under adversity.\n\nThis post shares the patterns that make production AI agents reliable: the DurableAgent pattern, circuit breakers, semantic recovery, exponential backoff, and persistent state management.\n\n## The Problem: Why Agents Fail in Production\n\n### Traditional Agent Architecture (Fragile)\n\n```python\ndef simple_agent(task: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"A typical agentic loop that looks good until it doesn't.\"\"\"\n    messages = [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": task}]\n    \n    for i in range(10):  # Max 10 steps\n        response = openai.ChatCompletion.create(\n            model=\"gpt-4\",\n            messages=messages\n        )\n        \n        assistant_msg = response.choices[0].message.content\n        messages.append({\"role\": \"assistant\", \"content\": assistant_msg})\n        \n        if \"DONE\" in assistant_msg:\n            return assistant_msg\n        \n        # Action execution (no error handling)\n        action_result = execute_action(assistant_msg)\n        messages.append({\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": action_result})\n    \n    return \"Max steps exceeded\"\n```\n\n**Failure modes:**\n1. **Network flake during LLM call** → entire state lost\n2. **Infinite loop** → agent spins until timeout\n3. **Hallucinated actions** → executes invalid commands\n4. **Rate limit** → crash without retry\n5. **State corruption** → agent can't resume\n6. **No observability** → black box failure\n\n### What Production Needs\n\nProduction agents require:\n- **Persistence**: Survive process crashes, save state to durable storage\n- **Idempotence**: Safely retry operations without duplicating effects\n- **Observability**: Log every decision, action, and failure for debugging\n- **Backoff**: Exponential retry with jitter for transient failures\n- **Timeout**: Detect and recover from stuck agents\n- **Semantic recovery**: Resume from where you left off, not from scratch\n- **Circuit breaking**: Stop retrying when it's futile\n\n## The DurableAgent Pattern\n\nThe DurableAgent is a wrapper that adds durability to any agent loop. It stores agent state to a database, provides checkpointing, and handles recovery.\n\n### Architecture Diagram\n\n```\n┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                        DurableAgent                           │\n├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                                                                │\n│  ┌────────────────────┐                                       │\n│  │  Load Checkpoint   │ ← Resume from last persisted state    │\n│  └────────────────────┘                                       │\n│           ↓                                                    │\n│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐                   │\n│  │  Agent Step (with circuit breaker)     │                   │\n│  │  - Get LLM response                    │                   │\n│  │  - Parse action                        │                   │\n│  │  - Validate action                     │                   │\n│  └────────────────────────────────────────┘                   │\n│           ↓                                                    │\n│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐                   │\n│  │  Execute Action (with idempotency)     │                   │\n│  │  - Check idempotency token             │                   │\n│  │  - Run action                          │                   │\n│  │  - Store result                        │                   │\n│  └────────────────────────────────────────┘                   │\n│           ↓                                                    │\n│  ┌────────────────────────────────────────┐                   │\n│  │  Save Checkpoint                       │                   │\n│  │  - Persist state to database           │                   │\n│  │  - Flush to disk                       │                   │\n│  └────────────────────────────────────────┘                   │\n│           ↓                                                    │\n│  Loop or Return                                                │\n│                                                                │\n└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n### Core Implementation\n\n```python\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nimport json\nimport hashlib\n\nclass AgentStatus(Enum):\n    PENDING = \"pending\"\n    IN_PROGRESS = \"in_progress\"\n    WAITING_ACTION = \"waiting_action\"\n    COMPLETED = \"completed\"\n    FAILED = \"failed\"\n    TIMEOUT = \"timeout\"\n\n@dataclass\nclass AgentStep:\n    \"\"\"One step in the agent's execution.\"\"\"\n    step_number: int\n    thought: str\n    action: str\n    action_input: Dict[str, Any]\n    observation: Optional[str] = None\n    timestamp: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    idempotency_key: str = \"\"\n\n@dataclass\nclass AgentCheckpoint:\n    \"\"\"Durable checkpoint of agent state.\"\"\"\n    agent_id: str\n    task: str\n    status: AgentStatus\n    steps: list[AgentStep] = field(default_factory=list)\n    current_messages: list[Dict[str, str]] = field(default_factory=list)\n    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)\n    created_at: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    last_updated: datetime = field(default_factory=datetime.utcnow)\n    failure_reason: Optional[str] = None\n    retry_count: int = 0\n    \n    def to_json(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Serialize checkpoint for storage.\"\"\"\n        return json.dumps({\n            \"agent_id\": self.agent_id,\n            \"task\": self.task,\n            \"status\": self.status.value,\n            \"steps\": [\n                {\n                    \"step_number\": s.step_number,\n                    \"thought\": s.thought,\n                    \"action\": s.action,\n                    \"action_input\": s.action_input,\n                    \"observation\": s.observation,\n                    \"timestamp\": s.timestamp.isoformat(),\n                    \"idempotency_key\": s.idempotency_key,\n                }\n                for s in self.steps\n            ],\n            \"current_messages\": self.current_messages,\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n            \"created_at\": self.created_at.isoformat(),\n            \"last_updated\": self.last_updated.isoformat(),\n            \"failure_reason\": self.failure_reason,\n            \"retry_count\": self.retry_count,\n        })\n\nclass DurableAgent:\n    \"\"\"\n    A resilient agent wrapper that persists state and handles recovery.\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        agent_id: str,\n        model: str = \"gpt-4\",\n        max_steps: int = 20,\n        checkpoint_store=None,  # DB/Redis/S3 adapter\n        action_executor=None,\n        timeout_seconds: int = 600,\n    ):\n        self.agent_id = agent_id\n        self.model = model\n        self.max_steps = max_steps\n        self.checkpoint_store = checkpoint_store\n        self.action_executor = action_executor\n        self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds\n        self.circuit_breaker = CircuitBreaker(failure_threshold=3)\n        self.logger = setup_logger(f\"agent-{agent_id}\")\n    \n    def run(self, task: str, resume: bool = True) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Run the agent, optionally resuming from checkpoint.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Load checkpoint if resuming\n        checkpoint = None\n        if resume:\n            checkpoint = self.checkpoint_store.load(self.agent_id)\n        \n        if checkpoint is None:\n            checkpoint = AgentCheckpoint(\n                agent_id=self.agent_id,\n                task=task,\n                status=AgentStatus.PENDING,\n            )\n        elif checkpoint.status in [AgentStatus.COMPLETED, AgentStatus.FAILED]:\n            self.logger.info(f\"Agent already finished: {checkpoint.status.value}\")\n            return {\"status\": checkpoint.status.value, \"result\": checkpoint}\n        \n        # Check timeout\n        if self.is_timed_out(checkpoint):\n            checkpoint.status = AgentStatus.TIMEOUT\n            checkpoint.failure_reason = \"Agent execution timeout exceeded\"\n            self.checkpoint_store.save(checkpoint)\n            self.logger.error(\"Agent timeout, marking as failed\")\n            return {\"status\": \"timeout\", \"result\": checkpoint}\n        \n        checkpoint.status = AgentStatus.IN_PROGRESS\n        checkpoint.last_updated = datetime.utcnow()\n        \n        try:\n            while len(checkpoint.steps) < self.max_steps:\n                # Get LLM response\n                try:\n                    response = self._get_llm_response(checkpoint)\n                except Exception as e:\n                    if self.circuit_breaker.is_open():\n                        checkpoint.status = AgentStatus.FAILED\n                        checkpoint.failure_reason = f\"Circuit breaker open: {str(e)}\"\n                        self.checkpoint_store.save(checkpoint)\n                        self.logger.error(\"Circuit breaker opened, stopping agent\")\n                        return {\"status\": \"failed\", \"result\": checkpoint}\n                    \n                    # Retry with exponential backoff\n                    backoff_seconds = self._exponential_backoff(checkpoint.retry_count)\n                    self.logger.warning(\n                        f\"LLM error, retry {checkpoint.retry_count}, \"\n                        f\"backoff {backoff_seconds}s: {str(e)}\"\n                    )\n                    checkpoint.retry_count += 1\n                    checkpoint.last_updated = datetime.utcnow()\n                    self.checkpoint_store.save(checkpoint)\n                    # In real usage, you'd sleep or schedule a retry\n                    continue\n                \n                # Parse action from response\n                action, action_input = self._parse_action(response)\n                \n                # Create step\n                step = AgentStep(\n                    step_number=len(checkpoint.steps),\n                    thought=response,\n                    action=action,\n                    action_input=action_input,\n                    idempotency_key=self._generate_idempotency_key(\n                        checkpoint.agent_id, len(checkpoint.steps), action, action_input\n                    ),\n                )\n                \n                # Check for termination\n                if action == \"Finish\":\n                    step.observation = action_input.get(\"output\", \"\")\n                    checkpoint.steps.append(step)\n                    checkpoint.status = AgentStatus.COMPLETED\n                    checkpoint.last_updated = datetime.utcnow()\n                    self.checkpoint_store.save(checkpoint)\n                    self.logger.info(\"Agent completed successfully\")\n                    return {\"status\": \"completed\", \"result\": checkpoint}\n                \n                # Execute action idempotently\n                try:\n                    observation = self.action_executor.execute(\n                        action=action,\n                        input=action_input,\n                        idempotency_key=step.idempotency_key,\n                    )\n                    step.observation = observation\n                    self.circuit_breaker.record_success()\n                    checkpoint.retry_count = 0\n                except Exception as e:\n                    self.circuit_breaker.record_failure()\n                    step.observation = f\"Error: {str(e)}\"\n                    self.logger.error(f\"Action execution failed: {str(e)}\")\n                \n                # Save step\n                checkpoint.steps.append(step)\n                checkpoint.last_updated = datetime.utcnow()\n                self.checkpoint_store.save(checkpoint)\n                self.logger.info(\n                    f\"Step {step.step_number}: {step.action} → {step.observation[:100]}\"\n                )\n            \n            # Max steps exceeded\n            checkpoint.status = AgentStatus.FAILED\n            checkpoint.failure_reason = f\"Max steps ({self.max_steps}) exceeded\"\n            self.checkpoint_store.save(checkpoint)\n            self.logger.error(\"Agent hit max steps limit\")\n            return {\"status\": \"failed\", \"result\": checkpoint}\n        \n        except Exception as e:\n            checkpoint.status = AgentStatus.FAILED\n            checkpoint.failure_reason = str(e)\n            self.checkpoint_store.save(checkpoint)\n            self.logger.exception(\"Unexpected agent failure\")\n            return {\"status\": \"failed\", \"result\": checkpoint}\n    \n    def _get_llm_response(self, checkpoint: AgentCheckpoint) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get LLM response with retry logic.\"\"\"\n        messages = checkpoint.current_messages or [\n            {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": checkpoint.task}\n        ]\n        \n        # Reconstruct conversation from steps\n        for step in checkpoint.steps:\n            messages.append({\"role\": \"assistant\", \"content\": step.thought})\n            messages.append({\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": step.observation or \"\"})\n        \n        response = openai.ChatCompletion.create(\n            model=self.model,\n            messages=messages,\n            temperature=0,\n        )\n        \n        content = response.choices[0].message.content\n        checkpoint.current_messages = messages + [\n            {\"role\": \"assistant\", \"content\": content}\n        ]\n        return content\n    \n    def _parse_action(self, response: str) -> tuple[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"Extract action and inputs from LLM response.\"\"\"\n        # This is a simplified parser; use JSON mode in production\n        lines = response.split(\"\\n\")\n        for i, line in enumerate(lines):\n            if \"Action:\" in line:\n                action = line.replace(\"Action:\", \"\").strip()\n                if i + 1 < len(lines) and \"Action Input:\" in lines[i + 1]:\n                    input_str = lines[i + 1].replace(\"Action Input:\", \"\").strip()\n                    try:\n                        action_input = json.loads(input_str)\n                    except json.JSONDecodeError:\n                        action_input = {\"raw\": input_str}\n                    return action, action_input\n        \n        return \"Finish\", {\"output\": response}\n    \n    def _generate_idempotency_key(\n        self, agent_id: str, step: int, action: str, action_input: Dict\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate deterministic idempotency key for this step.\"\"\"\n        data = f\"{agent_id}:{step}:{action}:{json.dumps(action_input, sort_keys=True)}\"\n        return hashlib.sha256(data.encode()).hexdigest()\n    \n    def _exponential_backoff(self, retry_count: int) -> float:\n        \"\"\"Calculate exponential backoff with jitter.\"\"\"\n        import random\n        base = 2\n        backoff = min(base ** retry_count, 300)  # Cap at 5 minutes\n        jitter = random.uniform(0, backoff * 0.1)\n        return backoff + jitter\n    \n    def is_timed_out(self, checkpoint: AgentCheckpoint) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if agent has exceeded timeout.\"\"\"\n        elapsed = (datetime.utcnow() - checkpoint.created_at).total_seconds()\n        return elapsed > self.timeout_seconds\n\n\nclass CircuitBreaker:\n    \"\"\"Prevent cascading failures by stopping retries when error rate is high.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, failure_threshold: int = 5, timeout_seconds: int = 60):\n        self.failure_threshold = failure_threshold\n        self.timeout_seconds = timeout_seconds\n        self.failure_count = 0\n        self.last_failure_time = None\n        self.state = \"closed\"  # closed, open, half-open\n    \n    def is_open(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if circuit is open (no more retries).\"\"\"\n        if self.state == \"closed\":\n            return False\n        \n        # Half-open: allow retry after timeout\n        if self.state == \"half-open\":\n            return False\n        \n        # Open: check timeout\n        if self.last_failure_time:\n            elapsed = (datetime.utcnow() - self.last_failure_time).total_seconds()\n            if elapsed > self.timeout_seconds:\n                self.state = \"half-open\"\n                return False\n        \n        return True\n    \n    def record_success(self):\n        \"\"\"Record successful call, reset circuit.\"\"\"\n        self.failure_count = 0\n        self.state = \"closed\"\n    \n    def record_failure(self):\n        \"\"\"Record failed call, potentially open circuit.\"\"\"\n        self.failure_count += 1\n        self.last_failure_time = datetime.utcnow()\n        if self.failure_count >= self.failure_threshold:\n            self.state = \"open\"\n```\n\n## Recovery Patterns\n\n### Pattern 1: Idempotent Actions\n\nActions must be idempotent—safe to retry without side effects.\n\n```python\nclass IdempotentActionExecutor:\n    \"\"\"Execute actions only once per step.\"\"\"\n    \n    def __init__(self, result_store=None):  # DB adapter\n        self.result_store = result_store\n    \n    def execute(self, action: str, input: Dict, idempotency_key: str):\n        \"\"\"Execute action, using cached result if it exists.\"\"\"\n        # Check if we already executed this step\n        cached_result = self.result_store.get(idempotency_key)\n        if cached_result is not None:\n            return cached_result\n        \n        # Execute the action\n        result = self._execute_unsafe(action, input)\n        \n        # Cache result\n        self.result_store.set(idempotency_key, result)\n        return result\n    \n    def _execute_unsafe(self, action: str, input: Dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Execute without idempotency guarantee.\"\"\"\n        if action == \"CreateResource\":\n            return self._create_resource(input)\n        elif action == \"QueryDatabase\":\n            return self._query_database(input)\n        # ... other actions\n```\n\n### Pattern 2: Semantic Recovery\n\nInstead of replaying the entire agent, provide context of completed work.\n\n```python\ndef resume_agent_with_context(agent_id: str, checkpoint: AgentCheckpoint):\n    \"\"\"Resume agent, providing summary of completed work.\"\"\"\n    \n    # Build context of completed work\n    work_summary = \"\\n\".join([\n        f\"Step {i+1}: {step.action}\\n  Input: {step.action_input}\\n  Result: {step.observation}\"\n        for i, step in enumerate(checkpoint.steps[-3:])  # Last 3 steps for context\n    ])\n    \n    # Add to system message\n    context_message = f\"\"\"\nYou are resuming from a previous agent execution. Here's what was already done:\n\n{work_summary}\n\nContinue from where you left off. Do NOT repeat these steps.\n    \"\"\"\n    \n    messages = [\n        {\"role\": \"system\", \"content\": context_message},\n        {\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": checkpoint.task},\n    ]\n    \n    return agent.run_with_messages(messages)\n```\n\n## Production Checklist\n\nDeploying production agents requires:\n\n- ✅ **Persistence layer**: Database, Redis, S3 for checkpoints\n- ✅ **Idempotency store**: Track executed actions\n- ✅ **Circuit breaker**: Prevent cascading failures\n- ✅ **Exponential backoff**: Smart retry with jitter\n- ✅ **Timeouts**: Detect stuck agents\n- ✅ **Observability**: Log every decision and action\n- ✅ **Dead letter queue**: Store failed agents for investigation\n- ✅ **Monitoring**: Alert on error rate, step latency, timeout rate\n- ✅ **Graceful shutdown**: Persist state before terminating\n\n## Conclusion\n\nProduction AI agents are not simple loops—they require durability, idempotency, observability, and recovery. The DurableAgent pattern provides a framework for building resilient agents that survive failures, resume work, and maintain correctness at scale.\n\nThe key insight: **durability is not a feature, it's a foundation.** Build on top of it.\n\n---\n\n**Next Steps:**\n- Implement checkpoint storage (PostgreSQL, Redis, S3)\n- Add monitoring and alerting\n- Test failure scenarios (network flakes, timeouts, hallucinations)\n- Build observability dashboards\n"

Will escalate to the user if the model proposes a command that is not in the\n            \"trusted\" set\n          - on-failure: Run all commands without asking for user approval. Only asks for approval if\n            a command fails to execute, in which case it will escalate to the user to ask for\n            un-sandboxed execution\n          - on-request: The model decides when to ask the user for approval\n          - never:      Never ask for user approval Execution failures are immediately returned to\n            the model\n\n      --full-auto\n          Convenience alias for low-friction sandboxed automatic execution (-a on-request, --sandbox\n          workspace-write)\n\n      --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox\n          Skip all confirmation prompts and execute commands without sandboxing. EXTREMELY\n          DANGEROUS. Intended solely for running in environments that are externally sandboxed\n\n  -C, --cd <DIR>\n          Tell the agent to use the specified directory as its working root\n\n      --search\n          Enable web search (off by default). When enabled, the native Responses `web_search` tool\n          is available to the model (no per‑call approval)\n\n      --add-dir <DIR>\n          Additional directories that should be writable alongside the primary workspace\n\n  -h, --help\n          Print help (see a summary with '-h')\n\n  -V, --version\n          Print version\n",

Then create a `<div id=\"terminal\"></div>` onto which xterm can attach itself. Finally instantiate the `Terminal` object and then call the `open` function with the DOM object of the `div`.\n\n```\n<!doctype html>\n  <html>\n    <head>\n      <link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"node_modules/@xterm/xterm/css/xterm.css\" />\n      <script src=\"node_modules/@xterm/xterm/lib/xterm.js\"></script>\n    </head>\n    <body>\n      <div id=\"terminal\"></div>\n      <script>\n        var term = new Terminal();\n        term.open(document.getElementById('terminal'));\n        term.write('Hello from \\x1B[1;3;31mxterm.js\\x1B[0m $ ')\n      </script>\n    </body>\n  </html>\n```\n\n## Real-world uses\n\nXterm.js is used in several world-class applications to provide great terminal experiences.\n\nReal-world uses\n\n-   [**SourceLair**](https://www.sourcelair.com/): In-browser IDE that provides its users with fully-featured Linux terminals based on xterm.js.\n-   [**Microsoft Visual Studio Code**](http://code.visualstudio.com/): Modern, versatile, and powerful open source code editor that provides an integrated terminal based on xterm.js.\n-   [**ttyd**](https://github.com/tsl0922/ttyd): A command-line tool for sharing terminal over the web, with fully-featured terminal emulation based on xterm.js.\n-   [**Katacoda**](https://www.katacoda.com/): Katacoda is an Interactive Learning Platform for software developers, covering the latest Cloud Native technologies.\n-   [**Eclipse Che**](http://www.eclipse.org/che): Developer workspace server, cloud IDE, and Eclipse next-generation IDE.\n-   [**Codenvy**](http://www.codenvy.com): Cloud workspaces for development teams.\n-   [**CoderPad**](https://coderpad.io): Online interviewing platform for programmers. ?onlyin, limit = limit))]\npub fn search_files(query: &str, onlyin: Option<&str>, limit: usize) -> Vec<FileResult> {\n    debug!(\"Starting mdfind search\");\n\n    if query.is_empty() {\n        debug!(\"Empty query, returning empty results\");\n        return Vec::new();\n    }\n\n    // Convert user query to proper mdfind query (filename matching)\n    let mdquery = build_mdquery(query);\n    debug!(mdquery = %mdquery, \"Built mdfind query\");\n\n    let mut cmd = Command::new(\"mdfind\");\n\n    // Add -onlyin if specified\n    if let Some(dir) = onlyin {\n        cmd.arg(\"-onlyin\").arg(dir);\n    }\n\n    // Add the query\n    cmd.arg(&mdquery);\n\n    // Set up streaming: pipe stdout instead of buffering\n    cmd.stdout(Stdio::piped());\n    cmd.stderr(Stdio::piped());\n\n    debug!(command = ?cmd, \"Spawning mdfind\");\n\n    let mut child = match cmd.spawn() {\n        Ok(child) => child,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"Failed to spawn mdfind\");\n            return Vec::new();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Run the search with a timeout to prevent hanging on no-match queries\n    // mdfind can hang indefinitely when Spotlight index is being rebuilt or\n    // for queries that don't match anything\n    let results = search_with_timeout(&mut child, limit);\n\n    // Clean up the child process\n    let _ = child.kill();\n    let _ = child.wait();\n\n    debug!(result_count = results.len(), \"Search completed\");\n    results\n}\n\n/// Internal helper that reads results from mdfind with a timeout.\n/// Returns collected results when timeout expires or all results are read.\nfn search_with_timeout(child: &mut Child, limit: usize) -> Vec<FileResult> {\n    // Take stdout for streaming reads\n    let stdout = match child.stdout.take() {\n        Some(stdout) => stdout,\n        None => {\n            warn!(\"Failed to capture mdfind stdout\");\n            return Vec::new();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Spawn a thread to read results - this allows us to timeout\n    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();\n    std::thread::spawn(move || {\n        let reader = BufReader::new(stdout);\n        let mut results = Vec::new();\n\n        // Stream line-by-line, stopping after limit\n        for line_result in reader.lines() {\n            if results.len() >= limit {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            let line = match line_result {\n                Ok(line) => line,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    debug!(error = %e, \"Error reading mdfind output line\");\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Only skip truly empty lines, not lines with spaces\n            // NOTE: .lines() already strips newline characters (\\n, \\r\\n).\n            // We intentionally do NOT call trim() because macOS paths CAN contain\n            // leading/trailing spaces (rare but valid).\n            if line.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let path = Path::new(&line);\n\n            // Get file metadata\n            let (size, modified) = match std::fs::metadata(path) {\n                Ok(meta) => {\n                    let size = meta.len();\n                    let modified = meta\n                        .modified()\n                        .ok()\n                        .and_then(|t| t.duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).ok())\n                        .map(|d| d.as_secs())\n                        .unwrap_or(0);\n                    (size, modified)\n                }\n                Err(_) => (0, 0),\n            };\n\n            let name = path\n                .file_name()\n                .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"\")\n                .to_string();\n\n            let file_type = detect_file_type(path);\n\n            results.push(FileResult {\n                path: line,\n                name,\n                size,\n                modified,\n                file_type,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Send results (ignore error if receiver dropped due to timeout)\n        let _ = tx.send(results);\n    });\n\n    // Wait for results with timeout\n    match rx.recv_timeout(MDFIND_TIMEOUT) {\n        Ok(results) => results,\n        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => {\n            warn!(\n                timeout_secs = MDFIND_TIMEOUT.as_secs(),\n                \"mdfind search timed out - Spotlight may be slow or query has no matches\"\n            );\n            Vec::new()\n        }\n        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => {\n            warn!(\"mdfind reader thread disconnected unexpectedly\");\n            Vec::new()\n        }\n    }\n}"

/Users/johnlindquist/.local/share/opencode/storage/part/msg_ba46bbb1a001xIcPzRfrIXeXRx/prt_ba46bd248001FX83WF1La2hgwN.json:23:        "after": "//! File Search Module using macOS Spotlight (mdfind)\n//!\n//! This module provides file search functionality using macOS's mdfind command,\n//! which interfaces with the Spotlight index for fast file searching.\n\nuse std::io::{BufRead, BufReader};\nuse std::path::Path;\nuse std::process::{Child, Command, Stdio};\nuse std::sync::mpsc;\nuse std::time::{Duration, UNIX_EPOCH};\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument, warn};\n\n/// Timeout for mdfind searches. Spotlight can hang indefinitely for queries\n/// that don't match anything, so we need to timeout and return empty results.\nconst MDFIND_TIMEOUT: Duration = Duration::from_secs(5);\n\n/// File type classification based on extension\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]\npub enum FileType {\n    File,\n    Directory,\n    Application,\n    Image,\n    Document,\n    Audio,\n    Video,\n    #[default]\n    Other,\n}\n\n/// Information about a file for the actions dialog\n/// Used as context for file-specific actions (similar to PathInfo and ScriptInfo)\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct FileInfo {\n    /// Full path to the file\n    pub path: String,\n    /// File name (last component of path)\n    pub name: String,\n    /// Type of file (used by the actions builder for context-specific actions)\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub file_type: FileType,\n    /// Whether this is a directory\n    pub is_dir: bool,\n}\n\nimpl FileInfo {\n    /// Create FileInfo from a FileResult\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn from_result(result: &FileResult) -> Self {\n        FileInfo {\n            path: result.path.clone(),\n            name: result.name.clone(),\n            file_type: result.file_type,\n            is_dir: result.file_type == FileType::Directory,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create FileInfo from path string\n    #[allow(dead_code)]\n    pub fn from_path(path: &str) -> Self {\n        let path_obj = std::path::Path::new(path);\n        let name = path_obj\n            .file_name()\n            .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"\")\n            .to_string();\n        let is_dir = path_obj.is_dir();\n        let file_type = if is_dir {\n            FileType::Directory\n        } else {\n            FileType::File\n        };\n\n        FileInfo {\n            path: path.to_string(),\n            name,\n            file_type,\n            is_dir,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Result of a file search\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct FileResult {\n    /// Full path to the file\n    pub path: String,\n    /// File name (last component of path)\n    pub name: String,\n    /// File size in bytes\n    pub size: u64,\n    /// Last modified time as Unix timestamp\n    pub modified: u64,\n    /// Type of file\n    pub file_type: FileType,\n}\n\n/// Metadata for a single file\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub struct FileMetadata {\n    /// Full path to the file\n    pub path: String,\n    /// File name\n    pub name: String,\n    /// File size in bytes\n    pub size: u64,\n    /// Last modified time as Unix timestamp\n    pub modified: u64,\n    /// Type of file\n    pub file_type: FileType,\n    /// Whether the file is readable\n    pub readable: bool,\n    /// Whether the file is writable\n    pub writable: bool,\n}\n\n/// Default limit for UI display (final visible results after filtering)\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub const DEFAULT_LIMIT: usize = 50;\n\n/// Limit for interactive mdfind searches\n/// Smaller than directory listing because each result requires a stat() call\n/// 500 results is plenty for fuzzy filtering and keeps response time <1s\npub const DEFAULT_SEARCH_LIMIT: usize = 500;\n\n/// Default cache limit for directory listing (fast operation, can handle more)\n/// Directory listing is cheaper than mdfind search (single readdir vs many stat calls)\npub const DEFAULT_CACHE_LIMIT: usize = 2000;\n\n/// Check if the query looks like an advanced mdfind query (with operators)\n/// If so, pass it through directly; otherwise wrap as filename query\nfn looks_like_advanced_mdquery(q: &str) -> bool {\n    let q = q.trim();\n    q.contains(\"kMDItem\")\n        || q.contains(\"==\")\n        || q.contains(\"!=\")\n        || q.contains(\">=\")\n        || q.contains(\"<=\")\n        || q.contains(\"&&\")\n        || q.contains(\"||\")\n}\n\n/// Escape special characters for mdfind query string literals\nfn escape_md_string(s: &str) -> String {\n    s.replace('\\\\', \"\\\\\\\\\").replace('\"', \"\\\\\\\"\")\n}\n\n/// Build an mdfind query from user input\n/// - If input looks like advanced query syntax, pass through as-is\n/// - Otherwise, wrap as case-insensitive filename contains query\nfn build_mdquery(user_query: &str) -> String {\n    let q = user_query.trim();\n    if looks_like_advanced_mdquery(q) {\n        return q.to_string();\n    }\n    let escaped = escape_md_string(q);\n    format!(r#\"kMDItemFSName == \"*{}*\"c\"#, escaped)\n}\n\n// NOTE: escape_query() was removed because:\n// 1. It was unused dead code\n// 2. Command::new() does NOT use a shell, so shell escaping is irrelevant\n// 3. Arguments passed via .arg() are automatically handled safely\n\n/// Detect file type based on extension\nfn detect_file_type(path: &Path) -> FileType {\n    // Get extension first - we need it to check for .app bundles\n    let extension = path\n        .extension()\n        .and_then(|e| e.to_str())\n        .map(|e| e.to_lowercase());\n\n    // macOS .app bundles are directories but should be classified as Applications\n    // Check for .app extension BEFORE checking is_dir()\n    if extension.as_deref() == Some(\"app\") {\n        return FileType::Application;\n    }\n\n    // Check if it's a directory (but not an .app bundle)\n    if path.is_dir() {\n        return FileType::Directory;\n    }\n\n    match extension.as_deref() {\n        // Applications (already handled above, but kept for completeness)\n        Some(\"app\") => FileType::Application,\n\n        // Images\n        Some(\n            \"png\" | \"jpg\" | \"jpeg\" | \"gif\" | \"bmp\" | \"webp\" | \"svg\" | \"ico\" | \"tiff\" | \"heic\"\n            | \"heif\",\n        ) => FileType::Image,\n\n        // Documents\n        Some(\n            \"pdf\" | \"doc\" | \"docx\" | \"xls\" | \"xlsx\" | \"ppt\" | \"pptx\" | \"txt\" | \"rtf\" | \"odt\"\n            | \"ods\" | \"odp\" | \"pages\" | \"numbers\" | \"key\",\n        ) => FileType::Document,\n\n        // Audio\n        Some(\"mp3\" | \"wav\" | \"aac\" | \"flac\" | \"ogg\" | \"wma\" | \"m4a\" | \"aiff\") => FileType::Audio,\n\n        // Video\n        Some(\"mp4\" | \"mov\" | \"avi\" | \"mkv\" | \"wmv\" | \"flv\" | \"webm\" | \"m4v\" | \"mpeg\" | \"mpg\") => {\n            FileType::Video\n        }\n\n        // Check if it's a file (has extension but not matched above)\n        Some(_) => FileType::File,\n\n        // No extension - check if it exists to determine type\n        None => {\n            if path.exists() {\n                if path.is_dir() {\n                    FileType::Directory\n                } else {\n                    FileType::File\n                }\n            } else {\n                FileType::Other\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Search for files using macOS mdfind (Spotlight)\n///\n/// Uses streaming to avoid buffering all results when only `limit` are needed.\n/// Converts simple queries to filename-matching mdfind queries.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `query` - Search query string (will be converted to filename query if simple)\n/// * `onlyin` - Optional directory to limit search scope\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of results to return\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// Vector of FileResult structs containing file information\n///\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query = %query, onlyin = ?onlyin, limit = limit))]\npub fn search_files(query: &str, onlyin: Option<&str>, limit: usize) -> Vec<FileResult> {\n    debug!(\"Starting mdfind search\");\n\n    if query.is_empty() {\n        debug!(\"Empty query, returning empty results\");\n        return Vec::new();\n    }\n\n    // Convert user query to proper mdfind query (filename matching)\n    let mdquery = build_mdquery(query);\n    debug!(mdquery = %mdquery, \"Built mdfind query\");\n\n    let mut cmd = Command::new(\"mdfind\");\n\n    // Add -onlyin if specified\n    if let Some(dir) = onlyin {\n        cmd.arg(\"-onlyin\").arg(dir);\n    }\n\n    // Add the query\n    cmd.arg(&mdquery);\n\n    // Set up streaming: pipe stdout instead of buffering\n    cmd.stdout(Stdio::piped());\n    cmd.stderr(Stdio::piped());\n\n    debug!(command = ?cmd, \"Spawning mdfind\");\n\n    let mut child = match cmd.spawn() {\n        Ok(child) => child,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"Failed to spawn mdfind\");\n            return Vec::new();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Run the search with a timeout to prevent hanging on no-match queries\n    // mdfind can hang indefinitely when Spotlight index is being rebuilt or\n    // for queries that don't match anything\n    let results = search_with_timeout(&mut child, limit);\n\n    // Clean up the child process\n    let _ = child.kill();\n    let _ = child.wait();\n\n    debug!(result_count = results.len(), \"Search completed\");\n    results\n}\n\n/// Internal helper that reads results from mdfind with a timeout.\n/// Returns collected results when timeout expires or all results are read.\nfn search_with_timeout(child: &mut Child, limit: usize) -> Vec<FileResult> {\n    // Take stdout for streaming reads\n    let stdout = match child.stdout.take() {\n        Some(stdout) => stdout,\n        None => {\n            warn!(\"Failed to capture mdfind stdout\");\n            return Vec::new();\n        }\n    };\n\n    // Spawn a thread to read results - this allows us to timeout\n    let (tx, rx) = mpsc::channel();\n    std::thread::spawn(move || {\n        let reader = BufReader::new(stdout);\n        let mut results = Vec::new();\n\n        // Stream line-by-line, stopping after limit\n        for line_result in reader.lines() {\n            if results.len() >= limit {\n                break;\n            }\n\n            let line = match line_result {\n                Ok(line) => line,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    debug!(error = %e, \"Error reading mdfind output line\");\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Only skip truly empty lines, not lines with spaces\n            // NOTE: .lines() already strips newline characters (\\n, \\r\\n).\n            // We intentionally do NOT call trim() because macOS paths CAN contain\n            // leading/trailing spaces (rare but valid).\n            if line.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let path = Path::new(&line);\n\n            // Get file metadata\n            let (size, modified) = match std::fs::metadata(path) {\n                Ok(meta) => {\n                    let size = meta.len();\n                    let modified = meta\n                        .modified()\n                        .ok()\n                        .and_then(|t| t.duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).ok())\n                        .map(|d| d.as_secs())\n                        .unwrap_or(0);\n                    (size, modified)\n                }\n                Err(_) => (0, 0),\n            };\n\n            let name = path\n                .file_name()\n                .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"\")\n                .to_string();\n\n            let file_type = detect_file_type(path);\n\n            results.push(FileResult {\n                path: line,\n                name,\n                size,\n                modified,\n                file_type,\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Send results (ignore error if receiver dropped due to timeout)\n        let _ = tx.send(results);\n    });\n\n    // Wait for results with timeout\n    match rx.recv_timeout(MDFIND_TIMEOUT) {\n        Ok(results) => results,\n        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Timeout) => {\n            warn!(\n                timeout_secs = MDFIND_TIMEOUT.as_secs(),\n                \"mdfind search timed out - Spotlight may be slow or query has no matches\"\n            );\n            Vec::new()\n        }\n        Err(mpsc::RecvTimeoutError::Disconnected) => {\n            warn!(\"mdfind reader thread disconnected unexpectedly\");\n            Vec::new()\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get detailed metadata for a specific file\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `path` - Path to the file\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// Some(FileMetadata) if the file exists and is readable, None otherwise\n///\n#[allow(dead_code)]\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(path = %path))]\npub fn get_file_metadata(path: &str) -> Option<FileMetadata> {\n    debug!(\"Getting file metadata\");\n\n    let path_obj = Path::new(path);\n\n    let metadata = match std::fs::metadata(path_obj) {\n        Ok(m) => m,\n        Err(e) => {\n            debug!(error = %e, \"Failed to get file metadata\");\n            return None;\n        }\n    };\n\n    let name = path_obj\n        .file_name()\n        .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n        .to_string();\n\n    let size = metadata.len();\n\n    let modified = metadata\n        .modified()\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|t| t.duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).ok())\n        .map(|d| d.as_secs())\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    let file_type = if metadata.is_dir() {\n        FileType::Directory\n    } else {\n        detect_file_type(path_obj)\n    };\n\n    // Check permissions\n    let readable = path_obj.exists(); // If we got metadata, it's readable\n    let writable = !metadata.permissions().readonly();\n\n    Some(FileMetadata {\n        path: path.to_string(),\n        name,\n        size,\n        modified,\n        file_type,\n        readable,\n        writable,\n    })\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// UI Helper Functions\n// These functions are prepared for file search UI that's being implemented.\n// Allow dead_code temporarily until the file search view is complete.\n// ============================================================================\n\n/// Get an emoji icon for the file type (used in file search UI)\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn file_type_icon(file_type: FileType) -> &'static str {\n    match file_type {\n        FileType::Directory => \"📁\",\n        FileType::Application => \"📦\",\n        FileType::Image => \"🖼️\",\n        FileType::Document => \"📄\",\n        FileType::Audio => \"🎵\",\n        FileType::Video => \"🎬\",\n        FileType::File => \"📃\",\n        FileType::Other => \"📎\",\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format file size in human-readable format (e.g., \"1.2 MB\", \"456 KB\")\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn format_file_size(bytes: u64) -> String {\n    const KB: u64 = 1024;\n    const MB: u64 = KB * 1024;\n    const GB: u64 = MB * 1024;\n\n    if bytes >= GB {\n        format!(\"{:.1} GB\", bytes as f64 / GB as f64)\n    } else if bytes >= MB {\n        format!(\"{:.1} MB\", bytes as f64 / MB as f64)\n    } else if bytes >= KB {\n        format!(\"{:.1} KB\", bytes as f64 / KB as f64)\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{} B\", bytes)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format Unix timestamp as relative time (e.g., \"2 hours ago\", \"3 days ago\")\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn format_relative_time(unix_timestamp: u64) -> String {\n    use std::time::{SystemTime, UNIX_EPOCH};\n\n    let now = SystemTime::now()\n        .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n        .map(|d| d.as_secs())\n        .unwrap_or(0);\n\n    if unix_timestamp == 0 {\n        return \"Unknown\".to_string();\n    }\n\n    let diff = now.saturating_sub(unix_timestamp);\n\n    const MINUTE: u64 = 60;\n    const HOUR: u64 = MINUTE * 60;\n    const DAY: u64 = HOUR * 24;\n    const WEEK: u64 = DAY * 7;\n    const MONTH: u64 = DAY * 30;\n    const YEAR: u64 = DAY * 365;\n\n    if diff < MINUTE {\n        \"Just now\".to_string()\n    } else if diff < HOUR {\n        let mins = diff / MINUTE;\n        format!(\"{} min{} ago\", mins, if mins == 1 { \"\" } else { \"s\" })\n    } else if diff < DAY {\n        let hours = diff / HOUR;\n        format!(\"{} hour{} ago\", hours, if hours == 1 { \"\" } else { \"s\" })\n    } else if diff < WEEK {\n        let days = diff / DAY;\n        format!(\"{} day{} ago\", days, if days == 1 { \"\" } else { \"s\" })\n    } else if diff < MONTH {\n        let weeks = diff / WEEK;\n        format!(\"{} week{} ago\", weeks, if weeks == 1 { \"\" } else { \"s\" })\n    } else if diff < YEAR {\n        let months = diff / MONTH;\n        format!(\"{} month{} ago\", months, if months == 1 { \"\" } else { \"s\" })\n    } else {\n        let years = diff / YEAR;\n        format!(\"{} year{} ago\", years, if years == 1 { \"\" } else { \"s\" })\n    }\n}\n\n/// Open a file with the system default application\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn open_file(path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n    use std::process::Command;\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"macos\")]\n    {\n        Command::new(\"open\")\n            .arg(path)\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to open file: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n    {\n        Command::new(\"xdg-open\")\n            .arg(path)\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to open file: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"windows\")]\n    {\n        Command::new(\"cmd\")\n            .args([\"/C\", \"start\", \"\", path])\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to open file: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Reveal a file in Finder (macOS) or file manager\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn reveal_in_finder(path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n    use std::process::Command;\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"macos\")]\n    {\n        Command::new(\"open\")\n            .args([\"-R\", path])\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to reveal file: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"linux\")]\n    {\n        // Try to get the parent directory and open it\n        let parent = std::path::Path::new(path)\n            .parent()\n            .map(|p| p.to_string_lossy().to_string())\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| path.to_string());\n        Command::new(\"xdg-open\")\n            .arg(&parent)\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to reveal file: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"windows\")]\n    {\n        Command::new(\"explorer\")\n            .args([\"/select,\", path])\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to reveal file: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Preview a file using Quick Look (macOS)\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn quick_look(path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n    use std::process::Command;\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"macos\")]\n    {\n        Command::new(\"qlmanage\")\n            .args([\"-p\", path])\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to preview file: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(not(target_os = \"macos\"))]\n    {\n        // Quick Look is macOS-only; fall back to opening the file\n        open_file(path)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Show the \"Open With\" dialog for a file (macOS)\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn open_with(path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n    use std::process::Command;\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"macos\")]\n    {\n        // Use AppleScript to trigger the \"Open With\" menu\n        let script = format!(\n            r#\"tell application \"Finder\"\n                activate\n                set theFile to POSIX file \"{}\"\n                open information window of theFile\n            end tell\"#,\n            path.replace('\"', r#\"\\\"\"#)\n        );\n        Command::new(\"osascript\")\n            .args([\"-e\", &script])\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to open 'Open With' dialog: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(not(target_os = \"macos\"))]\n    {\n        let _ = path;\n        Err(\"Open With is only supported on macOS\".to_string())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Show the Get Info window for a file in Finder (macOS)\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn show_info(path: &str) -> Result<(), String> {\n    use std::process::Command;\n\n    #[cfg(target_os = \"macos\")]\n    {\n        // Use AppleScript to open the Get Info window\n        let script = format!(\n            r#\"tell application \"Finder\"\n                activate\n                set theFile to POSIX file \"{}\"\n                open information window of theFile\n            end tell\"#,\n            path.replace('\"', r#\"\\\"\"#)\n        );\n        Command::new(\"osascript\")\n            .args([\"-e\", &script])\n            .spawn()\n            .map_err(|e| format!(\"Failed to show file info: {}\", e))?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(not(target_os = \"macos\"))]\n    {\n        let _ = path;\n        Err(\"Show Info is only supported on macOS\".to_string())\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Path Navigation Helpers\n// These are pure string manipulation helpers for directory navigation UI.\n// They work with display paths (containing ~ or relative paths) without IO.\n// ============================================================================\n\n/// Ensure a path string ends with a trailing slash\n///\n/// Used to normalize directory paths for consistent display and navigation.\n///\n/// # Examples\n/// - `/foo/bar` → `/foo/bar/`\n/// - `~/dev/` → `~/dev/` (unchanged)\n/// - `` → `/` (empty becomes root)\n/// - `~` → `~/`\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn ensure_trailing_slash(path: &str) -> String {\n    if path.is_empty() {\n        return \"/\".to_string();\n    }\n    if path.ends_with('/') {\n        path.to_string()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{}/\", path)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Get the parent directory path for Shift+Tab navigation\n///\n/// This is a pure string operation for display paths. It handles:\n/// - Tilde paths (`~/foo/` → `~/`)\n/// - Absolute paths (`/foo/bar/` → `/foo/`)\n/// - Relative paths (`./` → `../`, `../` → `../../`)\n///\n/// Returns `None` for root paths that have no parent:\n/// - `/` (filesystem root)\n/// - `~/` (home directory root)\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `dir_with_slash` - Directory path (ideally ending with `/`, but handles without)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Some(parent_path)` - Parent directory path ending with `/`\n/// * `None` - If this is a root path with no parent\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn parent_dir_display(dir_with_slash: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    // Normalize: ensure we're working with a trailing-slash path\n    let normalized = if dir_with_slash.ends_with('/') {\n        dir_with_slash.to_string()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{}/\", dir_with_slash)\n    };\n\n    // Handle root cases that have no parent\n    if normalized == \"/\" || normalized == \"~/\" {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Handle relative paths specially\n    if normalized == \"./\" {\n        // Current dir -> parent dir\n        return Some(\"../\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    if normalized == \"../\" {\n        // One level up -> two levels up\n        return Some(\"../../\".to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Handle ../ chains: ../../ -> ../../../\n    if normalized.starts_with(\"../\") {\n        // Count existing ../ segments and add one more\n        return Some(format!(\"../{}\", normalized));\n    }\n\n    // For regular paths (absolute or tilde), find the parent by removing last segment\n    // e.g., \"/foo/bar/\" -> \"/foo/\", \"~/dev/kit/\" -> \"~/dev/\"\n\n    // Remove trailing slash for easier processing\n    let without_trailing = normalized.trim_end_matches('/');\n\n    // Find the last slash (which separates parent from current dir)\n    if let Some(last_slash_pos) = without_trailing.rfind('/') {\n        // Special case: tilde prefix\n        if without_trailing.starts_with(\"~/\") {\n            if last_slash_pos == 1 {\n                // \"~/foo\" -> last_slash at 1 -> parent is \"~/\"\n                return Some(\"~/\".to_string());\n            }\n            // \"~/foo/bar\" -> parent is \"~/foo/\"\n            return Some(format!(\"{}/\", &without_trailing[..last_slash_pos]));\n        }\n\n        // Absolute path case\n        if last_slash_pos == 0 {\n            // \"/foo\" -> parent is \"/\"\n            return Some(\"/\".to_string());\n        }\n\n        // General case: \"/foo/bar\" -> \"/foo/\"\n        return Some(format!(\"{}/\", &without_trailing[..last_slash_pos]));\n    }\n\n    // No slash found - shouldn't happen for valid directory paths\n    None\n}\n\n/// Shorten a path for display by using ~ for home directory\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn shorten_path(path: &str) -> String {\n    if let Some(home) = dirs::home_dir() {\n        if let Some(home_str) = home.to_str() {\n            if let Some(stripped) = path.strip_prefix(home_str) {\n                return format!(\"~{}\", stripped);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    path.to_string()\n}\n\n/// Expand a path string, replacing ~ with the home directory\n/// and resolving relative paths (., ..)\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `path` - Path string that may contain ~, ., or ..\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// Expanded absolute path as a String, or None if expansion fails\npub fn expand_path(path: &str) -> Option<String> {\n    let trimmed = path.trim();\n\n    if trimmed.is_empty() {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Handle home directory expansion\n    if trimmed == \"~\" {\n        return dirs::home_dir().and_then(|p| p.to_str().map(|s| s.to_string()));\n    }\n\n    if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix(\"~/\") {\n        return dirs::home_dir().and_then(|home| home.join(rest).to_str().map(|s| s.to_string()));\n    }\n\n    // Handle relative paths\n    if trimmed == \".\" || trimmed.starts_with(\"./\") {\n        let cwd = std::env::current_dir().ok()?;\n        let suffix = trimmed.strip_prefix(\"./\").unwrap_or(\"\");\n        if suffix.is_empty() {\n            return cwd.to_str().map(|s| s.to_string());\n        }\n        return cwd.join(suffix).to_str().map(|s| s.to_string());\n    }\n\n    if trimmed == \"..\" || trimmed.starts_with(\"../\") {\n        let cwd = std::env::current_dir().ok()?;\n        let parent = cwd.parent()?;\n        let suffix = trimmed.strip_prefix(\"../\").unwrap_or(\"\");\n        if suffix.is_empty() {\n            return parent.to_str().map(|s| s.to_string());\n        }\n        return parent.join(suffix).to_str().map(|s| s.to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Already an absolute path\n    if trimmed.starts_with('/') {\n        return Some(trimmed.to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Not a recognized path format\n    None\n}\n\n/// Internal cap to prevent runaway directory listings\nconst MAX_DIRECTORY_ENTRIES: usize = 5000;\n\n/// List contents of a directory\n///\n/// Returns files and directories sorted with directories first, then by name.\n/// Handles ~ expansion and relative paths.\n///\n/// NOTE: Does NOT truncate results. Callers should apply their own limit\n/// after scoring/filtering. An internal cap of 5000 entries prevents runaway.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `dir_path` - Directory path (can include ~, ., ..)\n/// * `_limit` - DEPRECATED: No longer used (kept for API compatibility)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// Vector of FileResult structs for directory contents\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(dir_path = %dir_path, limit = _limit))]\npub fn list_directory(dir_path: &str, _limit: usize) -> Vec<FileResult> {\n    debug!(\"Starting directory listing\");\n\n    // Expand the path\n    let expanded = match expand_path(dir_path) {\n        Some(p) => p,\n        None => {\n            debug!(\"Failed to expand path: {}\", dir_path);\n            return Vec::new();\n        }\n    };\n\n    let path = Path::new(&expanded);\n\n    // Check if it's a valid directory\n    if !path.is_dir() {\n        debug!(\"Path is not a directory: {}\", expanded);\n        return Vec::new();\n    }\n\n    // Read directory contents\n    let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(path) {\n        Ok(entries) => entries,\n        Err(e) => {\n            warn!(error = %e, \"Failed to read directory: {}\", expanded);\n            return Vec::new();\n        }\n    };\n\n    let mut results: Vec<FileResult> = Vec::new();\n\n    for entry in entries.flatten() {\n        let entry_path = entry.path();\n        let path_str = match entry_path.to_str() {\n            Some(s) => s.to_string(),\n            None => continue,\n        };\n\n        let name = entry_path\n            .file_name()\n            .and_then(|n| n.to_str())\n            .unwrap_or(\"\")\n            .to_string();\n\n        // Skip hidden files (starting with .)\n        if name.starts_with('.') {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Get metadata\n        let (size, modified) = match std::fs::metadata(&entry_path) {\n            Ok(meta) => {\n                let size = meta.len();\n                let modified = meta\n                    .modified()\n                    .ok()\n                    .and_then(|t| t.duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).ok())\n                    .map(|d| d.as_secs())\n                    .unwrap_or(0);\n                (size, modified)\n            }\n            Err(_) => (0, 0),\n        };\n\n        let file_type = detect_file_type(&entry_path);\n\n        results.push(FileResult {\n            path: path_str,\n            name,\n            size,\n            modified,\n            file_type,\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Sort: directories first, then alphabetically by name\n    results.sort_by(|a, b| {\n        let a_is_dir = matches!(a.file_type, FileType::Directory);\n        let b_is_dir = matches!(b.file_type, FileType::Directory);\n\n        match (a_is_dir, b_is_dir) {\n            (true, false) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,\n            (false, true) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,\n            _ => a.name.to_lowercase().cmp(&b.name.to_lowercase()),\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Apply internal cap to prevent runaway (callers truncate after filtering)\n    if results.len() > MAX_DIRECTORY_ENTRIES {\n        results.truncate(MAX_DIRECTORY_ENTRIES);\n    }\n\n    debug!(result_count = results.len(), \"Directory listing completed\");\n    results\n}\n\n/// Check if a path looks like a directory path that should be listed\n/// (as opposed to a search query)\n///\n/// Re-exports from input_detection module for convenience\npub use crate::scripts::input_detection::is_directory_path;\n\n/// Result of parsing a directory path with potential filter\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub struct ParsedDirPath {\n    /// The directory to list (always ends with / after expansion)\n    pub directory: String,\n    /// Optional filter pattern (the part after the last /)\n    pub filter: Option<String>,\n}\n\n/// Parse a directory path into its directory component and optional filter\n///\n/// This handles paths like:\n/// - `~/dev/` -> directory=`~/dev/`, filter=None (list all)\n/// - `~/dev/fin` -> directory=`~/dev/`, filter=Some(\"fin\") (filter by \"fin\")\n/// - `~/dev/mcp-` -> directory=`~/dev/`, filter=Some(\"mcp-\") (filter by \"mcp-\")\n/// - `/usr/local/bin` -> directory=`/usr/local/`, filter=Some(\"bin\")\n/// - `~` -> directory=`~`, filter=None\n///\n/// Returns None if:\n/// - The path doesn't look like a directory path\n/// - The parent directory doesn't exist\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(path = %path))]\npub fn parse_directory_path(path: &str) -> Option<ParsedDirPath> {\n    let trimmed = path.trim();\n\n    // Must be a directory-style path\n    if !is_directory_path(trimmed) {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Handle special case: just ~ or ~/ (home directory)\n    if trimmed == \"~\" || trimmed == \"~/\" {\n        return Some(ParsedDirPath {\n            directory: \"~\".to_string(),\n            filter: None,\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Handle paths ending with / - they're complete directory paths\n    if trimmed.ends_with('/') {\n        // Verify the directory exists\n        if let Some(expanded) = expand_path(trimmed.trim_end_matches('/')) {\n            let p = Path::new(&expanded);\n            if p.is_dir() {\n                return Some(ParsedDirPath {\n                    directory: trimmed.to_string(),\n                    filter: None,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    // Path doesn't end with / - split into parent dir and potential filter\n    // e.g., ~/dev/fin -> ~/dev/ + fin\n    if let Some(last_slash_idx) = trimmed.rfind('/') {\n        let parent = &trimmed[..=last_slash_idx]; // Include the slash\n        let potential_filter = &trimmed[last_slash_idx + 1..];\n\n        // Verify parent directory exists\n        let parent_to_check = if parent == \"/\" {\n            \"/\"\n        } else {\n            parent.trim_end_matches('/')\n        };\n\n        if let Some(expanded) = expand_path(parent_to_check) {\n            let p = Path::new(&expanded);\n            if p.is_dir() {\n                let filter = if potential_filter.is_empty() {\n                    None\n                } else {\n                    Some(potential_filter.to_string())\n                };\n                return Some(ParsedDirPath {\n                    directory: parent.to_string(),\n                    filter,\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    None\n}\n\n/// List directory contents with optional filter applied\n///\n/// This combines directory listing with instant filtering for responsive UX.\n/// When the user types `~/dev/fin`, we list `~/dev/` and filter by \"fin\".\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `dir_path` - Directory path (can include ~, ., ..)\n/// * `filter` - Optional filter string to match against filenames\n/// * `limit` - Maximum number of results to return\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// Vector of FileResult structs matching the filter\n#[allow(dead_code)]\n#[instrument(skip_all, fields(dir_path = %dir_path, filter = ?filter, limit = limit))]\npub fn list_directory_filtered(\n    dir_path: &str,\n    filter: Option<&str>,\n    limit: usize,\n) -> Vec<FileResult> {\n    // First get all directory contents\n    let mut results = list_directory(dir_path, limit * 2); // Get more to filter from\n\n    // Apply filter if present\n    if let Some(filter_str) = filter {\n        let filter_lower = filter_str.to_lowercase();\n        results.retain(|r| r.name.to_lowercase().contains(&filter_lower));\n    }\n\n    // Apply limit after filtering\n    results.truncate(limit);\n    results\n}\n\n/// Filter and sort FileResults using Nucleo fuzzy matching\n///\n/// This function filters cached file results by fuzzy-matching the filter pattern\n/// against file names, then sorts by match score (higher = better match).\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `results` - Slice of FileResult to filter\n/// * `filter_pattern` - The pattern to fuzzy-match against file names\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// Vector of (original_index, FileResult, score) tuples, sorted by score descending\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn filter_results_with_nucleo(\n    results: &[FileResult],\n    filter_pattern: &str,\n) -> Vec<(usize, FileResult, u32)> {\n    use crate::scripts::NucleoCtx;\n\n    let mut nucleo = NucleoCtx::new(filter_pattern);\n    let mut scored: Vec<(usize, FileResult, u32)> = results\n        .iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .filter_map(|(idx, r)| nucleo.score(&r.name).map(|score| (idx, r.clone(), score)))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Sort by score descending (higher = better match)\n    scored.sort_by(|a, b| b.2.cmp(&a.2));\n\n    scored\n}\n\n/// Filter FileResults using Nucleo and return only (index, FileResult) pairs\n///\n/// This is a convenience wrapper for use in UI code where the score isn't needed.\n/// Results are pre-sorted by match quality.\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `results` - Slice of FileResult to filter\n/// * `filter_pattern` - The pattern to fuzzy-match against file names\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// Vector of (original_index, &FileResult) tuples, sorted by match quality\n#[allow(dead_code)]\npub fn filter_results_nucleo_simple<'a>(\n    results: &'a [FileResult],\n    filter_pattern: &str,\n) -> Vec<(usize, &'a FileResult)> {\n    use crate::scripts::NucleoCtx;\n\n    let mut nucleo = NucleoCtx::new(filter_pattern);\n    let mut scored: Vec<(usize, &FileResult, u32)> = results\n        .iter()\n        .enumerate()\n        .filter_map(|(idx, r)| nucleo.score(&r.name).map(|score| (idx, r, score)))\n        .collect();\n\n    // Sort by score descending (higher = better match)\n    scored.sort_by(|a, b| b.2.cmp(&a.2));\n\n    // Return without scores\n    scored.into_iter().map(|(idx, r, _)| (idx, r)).collect()\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    // ========================================================================\n    // Query Builder Tests\n    // assert_eq!(format_file_size(512), \"512 B\");\n        assert_eq!(format_file_size(1023), \"1023 B\");\n\n        // Kilobytes\n        assert_eq!(format_file_size(1024), \"1.0 KB\");\n .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)\n            .unwrap()\n            .as_secs();\n\n        // Just now (0 seconds ago)\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now), \"Just now\");\n\n        // Minutes ago\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now - 60), \"1 min ago\");\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now - 120), \"2 mins ago\");\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now - 59 * 60), \"59 mins ago\");\n\n        // Hours ago\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now - 3600), \"1 hour ago\");\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now - 7200), \"2 hours ago\");\n\n        // Days ago\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now - 86400), \"1 day ago\");\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(now - 172800), \"2 days ago\");\n\n        // Unknown (0 timestamp)\n        assert_eq!(format_relative_time(0), \"Unknown\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_shorten_path() {\n        // Test with a path that doesn't start with home\n Directory Navigation Tests\n    // ========================================================================\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_expand_path_home() {\n        // Test ~ expansion\n        if // ~/subdir\n            let expanded = expand_path(\"~/Documents\");\n            assert!(expanded.is_some());\n            assert!(expanded.unwrap().starts_with(home_str));\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_expand_path_absolute() {\n        // Absolute paths should pass through unchanged\n        assert_eq!(expand_path(\"/usr/local\"), Some(\"/usr/local\".to_string()));\n  #[test]\n    fn test_expand_path_relative_current() {\n        // Relative paths with .\n        let cwd = std::env::current_dir().unwrap();\n        let cwd_str = cwd.to_str().unwrap();\n\n expand_path(\"./src\");\n        assert!(expanded.is_some());\n        let expected = cwd.join(\"src\");\n        assert_eq!(expanded.unwrap(), expected.to_str().unwrap());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n list_directory(\"/tmp\", 50);\n\n        // If we have results, verify sorting\n        if results.len() >= 2 {\n            // Find first file (non-directory)\n            let first_file_idx = results\n                .iter()\n                .position(|r| !matches!(r.file_type, FileType::Directory));\n\n            // Find last directory\n            let last_dir_idx = results\n                .iter()\n                .rposition(|r| matches!(r.file_type, FileType::Directory));\n\n            // If we have both dirs and files, dirs should come first\n            if let (Some(first_file), Some(last_dir)) = (first_file_idx, last_dir_idx) {\n                assert!(\n                    last_dir < first_file,\n                    \"Directories should come before files\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_list_directory_limit() {\n        // limit parameter is deprecated - list_directory no longer truncates\n        // Callers should apply their own limit after filtering/scoring\n        // We just verify that it doesn't panic and returns reasonable results\n        let results = list_directory(\"/\", 3);\n        // Should return all entries (up to internal cap) not just 3\n        // The \"/\" directory typically has multiple entries\n        assert!(!results.is_empty(), \"Root directory should have entries\");\n        // Verify internal cap works (5000)\n        assert!(results.len() <= 5000, \"Should respect internal cap of 5000\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_list_directory_hides_dotfiles() {\n        // Hidden !result.name.starts_with('.'),\n                \"Should not include hidden files: {}\",\n                result.name\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn Filtering Tests\n    // ========================================================================\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filter_results_nucleo_empty_pattern() {\n        let results = vec![\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/apple.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"apple.txt\".to_string(),\n                size: 100,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::Document,\n            },\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/banana.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"banana.txt\".to_string(),\n                size: 200,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::Document,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        // Empty pattern with Nucleo matches everything (score 0)\n        // This is expected behavior - caller should check for empty pattern before calling\n        let filtered = filter_results_nucleo_simple(&results, \"\");\n        assert_eq!(filtered.len(), 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filter_results_nucleo_exact_match() {\n        let results = vec![\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/mcp-final.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"mcp-final\".to_string(),\n                size: 100,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::File,\n            },\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/definitions.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"definitions\".to_string(),\n                size: 200,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::File,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        // \"final\" should match \"mcp-final\" better than \"definitions\"\n        let filtered = filter_results_nucleo_simple(&results, \"final\");\n        assert!(!filtered.is_empty());\n        assert_eq!(filtered[0].1.name, \"mcp-final\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filter_results_nucleo_fuzzy_ordering() {\n        let results = vec![\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/define.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"define\".to_string(),\n                size: 100,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::File,\n            },\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/mcp-final.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"mcp-final\".to_string(),\n                size: 200,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::File,\n            },\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/final-test.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"final-test\".to_string(),\n                size: 300,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::File,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        // \"fin\" should fuzzy match both \"mcp-final\" and \"final-test\"\n        // Both should rank higher than \"define\" (which has f, i, n but not consecutive)\n        let filtered = filter_results_nucleo_simple(&results, \"fin\");\n\n        // Should have matches\n        assert!(!filtered.is_empty());\n\n        // \"final-test\" or \"mcp-final\" should be first (both have \"fin\" as prefix of \"final\")\n        let first_name = &filtered[0].1.name;\n        assert!(\n            first_name.contains(\"final\"),\n            \"Expected 'final' in first result, got: {}\",\n            first_name\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filter_results_nucleo_no_matches() {\n        let results = vec![\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/apple.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"apple\".to_string(),\n                size: 100,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::File,\n            },\n            FileResult {\n                path: \"/test/banana.txt\".to_string(),\n                name: \"banana\".to_string(),\n                size: 200,\n                modified: 0,\n                file_type: FileType::File,\n            },\n        ];\n\n        // \"xyz\" should not match anything\n        let filtered = filter_results_nucleo_simple(&results, \"xyz\");\n        assert!(filtered.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_filter_results_nucleo_case_insensitive() {\n        let results = vec![FileResult {\n            path: \"/test/MyDocument.txt\".to_string(),\n            name: \"MyDocument\".to_string(),\n            size: 100,\n            modified: 0,\n            file_type: FileType::Document,\n        }];\n\n        // Should match regardless of case\n        let filtered_lower = filter_results_nucleo_simple(&results, \"mydoc\");\n        let filtered_upper = filter_results_nucleo_simple(&results, \"MYDOC\");\n        let filtered_mixed = filter_results_nucleo_simple(&results, \"MyDoc\");\n\n        assert!(!filtered_lower.is_empty());\n        assert!(!filtered_upper.is_empty());\n        assert!(!filtered_mixed.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    // ========================================================================\n    // FileInfo Tests\n    // ========================================================================\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_file_info_from_result() {\n        let result = FileResult {\n            path: \"/test/document.pdf\".to_string(),\n            name: \"document.pdf\".to_string(),\n            size: 1024,\n            modified: 1234567890,\n            file_type: FileType::Document,\n        };\n\n        let info = FileInfo::from_result(&result);\n        assert_eq!(info.path, \"/test/document.pdf\");\n        assert_eq!(info.name, \"document.pdf\");\n        assert_eq!(info.file_type,    let info = FileInfo::from_result(&result);\n        assert_eq!(info.path, \"/test/Documents\");\n        assert_eq!(info.name, \"Documents\");\n        assert_eq!(info.file_type,
